520  
The Tenth Chapter: Dedication 
4. Explaining, by way of the branch of dedication, th e way to train in giving away body, material wealth, 
and the roots of virtue for the welfare of others 
A. Explaining the chapter’s material 
1) A brief presentation of the dedication for all migrators to engage in the bodhisattvas’ conduct 
2) An extensive explanation of the dedication 
a) Dedicating for the welfare of others 
i) Dedicating for mundane purposes 
1’ Dedicating so that the suffering of disease and so forth may be pacified 
a’ Dedicating for the sake of temporary pleasure 
b’ Dedicating for the sake of the final bliss 
2’ Dedicating so that the suffering of  the lower realms may be pacified 
a’ Pacifying the hells realms’ suffering 
1’’ Dedicating so that such suffering may quickly be pacified 
a’’ Dedicating so that the universal sufferings [of the hell realms] may be pacified 
b’’ Pacifying the sufferings of the cold hells 
c’’ Dedicating so that the sufferings of the hot hells may be pacified 
(1) Dedicating so that dharmas arisen from the clouds of the 
bodhisattvas’ two accumulations may pacify suffering 
(2) Pacifying the suffering of the adjoining hells 
(3) The actual site of the hells 
(4) Dedicating so that the other sufferings of the adjoining hells are 
pacified 
2’’ Dedicating so that it may be pacified through the force of others 
a’’ Pacifying suffering through the force of Vajrapani 
b’’ Pacifying suffering through the compassion of Avalokiteshvara 
c’’ Pacifying suffering through the force of Manjushri’s miraculous display 
d’’ Aspiring for pacification through the capacities of the other Closest Sons 
[Kshitigarbha, Akashagarbha, Maitreya, etc.] 
b’ Dedicating for the welfare of animals 
c’ Dedicating for the welfare of hungry ghosts 
3’ Dedicating for the welfare of [beings in] the higher realms 
a’ Dedicating so that they may be free of suffering 
1’’ Dedicating so that they may not be born with incomplete faculties and be without 
discomfort while in the womb 
2’’ Dedicating so that they are without the suffering of poverty and mental discomfort 
3’’ Dedicating so that they may be without the sufferings of illness and the suffering of 
encountering aversion 
4’’ Dedicating so that the suffering of beings in all directions and the harm caused by non-
humans is pacified 
b’ Dedicating so that they may accomplish their desired aims 
1’’ Dedicating for the welfare of humans in general 
a’’ Dedicating so that they may obtain excellences 
(1) Dedicating so that they obtain the support of leisures and endowments and 
excellent resources 
(2) Dedicating so that they are without violence and have the excellent magnificence 
(3) Dedicating so that they may have the excellent body and that the inferior become supreme 
(4) Dedicating for the excellent virtues 
b’’ Dedicating so that they enter the correct path 
(1) Wishing for them to have the excelle nt thought and action for accomplishing the 
path of liberation (2) Wishing that all conducive conditions be complete and that they be without 
adverse conditions 
c’’ Dedicating so that they have the temporary endowments and pleasure 
(1) Dedicating so that they have long lives and so that the world-this vessel- be pure 
and clean

IV. The Conclusion 
A. Who composed the treatise 
B. Who translated it 
 
We will recite Chapter X to dedicate the roots of  virtue and the merit we have acquired through 
studying and listening to this wisd om chapter of Shantideva's text. 
 
(Please refer to the translation of the root text by Toh Sze Gee for recitation of the dedication chapter ) 
 
Some of you may be feeling discouraged or disappo inted that, even after coming to class, you do 
not understand this topic, which is so difficult . You may feel there is no difference whether you 
attend class or not because you still do not unders tand anything. But this is not the case. Even if 
you do not understand everything, it  is definitely beneficial for you to come to class and listen to 
the teachings. It definitely makes a difference.   There are people who mentioned to me how much  they had benefited from attending classes. 
They used to find it so difficult to understand  books and texts on emptiness in the past. After 
attending these classes, they find these same mate rials so much easier to understand when they 
read them again. In fact, they are motivated to continue reading more about emptiness.  
 There are also people who said that,  precisel y because of having attended these classes and 
listening to the teachings, they are able to un derstand the teachings on emptiness that were 
recently given by His Holiness the Dalai Lama in  Dharamsala. Some studen ts told me that, due 
to coming to class, they found their interest in the teachings have increased and they have 
generated greater determination to take  their studies to the next level. 
 
Amitabha Buddhist Centre - Geshe Chonyi         Engaging in the Bodhisattva Deeds - Part 2 
FPMT Basic Program                     CHAPTER X - DEDICATION  
 
522  
I believe these people are merely expressing their own experiences. I do no t think they are out to 
make me happy. 
 
All this has happened because of the coming togeth er of all the conditions: there is a teacher, a 
translator and students. From my side, I am not sure whether all of you benefited from this 
module but I am quite sure that at least one or two persons definitely benefited. By coming to 
class, it increased their enthusiasm for their stud ies and their understanding of the importance of 
studying. Their interest in the Buddhadharma ha s become stronger. Even if only one or two 
persons experience this kind of benefit, I th ink that is a very good thing. I rejoice.  
 
Sometimes, I do question the point of teaching this complicated material  here when people are 
so busy and already have so many difficulties in th eir own lives. The text is tough. People did not 
seem to understand what I was trying to explain.  I wondered then what is the purpose of giving 
such teachings.   It was difficult for me, especially for this IXth chapter. Things must have been difficult for you 
too. Of course, from my side, I cannot really ex plain the topic of emptiness well but when I saw 
that a few people benefited from it, their unders tanding of emptiness improved a little and they 
were a little more encouraged and interested in their studies, my enthusiasm and determination 
was revived. From my side, I had completed my st udies. I always pray for those who are actually 
studying correctly to study well and to continue to put in effort in their studies. I always pray that 
they may be able to complete their studies. An yway, I had spoken on many occasions before on 
how extremely important it is to listen and to study the teachings. Relatively speaking, I had 
studied more than you, so from that point of view, I can tell you from my personal experience 
how extremely important it is.
